author: Athanasius of Alexandria
book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
Additional Note on Apol. C. Arianos, §50.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: The materials for an authentic list are (1) the names given by Athanasius, Apol. c.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: 50, previous to the lists of bishops from various provinces who signed the letter of the council when in circulation.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: These names, given with no specification of their sees, are 77 in number.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: (2) The list of signatures to the letter of the council to Julius, given by Hilary, Fragm. ii., 59 in number.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: The signatures to the letters discovered by Maffei and printed in Migne, Patr.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: Of these, 26 sign (3) the council’s letter to the Mareotic Churches, and 61, in part the same, sign (4) the letter of Athanasius to the same (Letter 46 in this volume).

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: These signatures comprise 30 names not given by Hilary, while those in (1) add six which are absent from (2) and (3) alike.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: We add (5) Gratus of Carthage, present according to the Greek text of the Canons, although he afterward signed the letter in a local council of his own, like Maximin of Treveri, Verissimus of Lyons, and Arius of Palestine, who are therefore given by Athanasius in his second list (the former two being omitted from the first): also Euphrates of Cologne, who was sent by Constans to Antioch with the council’s decisions (Prolegg. ch. ii. §6), and was therefore most likely present at the council itself.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: This total is confirmed if we subtract from the ‘170 more or less’ of Hist.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: 15 the 76 seceders to Philippopolis (Sabinus in Socr. ii.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: 16), 73 of whom sign their letter, given by Hilary.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: This leaves 94 ‘more or less,’ so that the list now to be given, in elucidation of that of Athanasius, has strong claims to rank as approximately correct.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: The numbers after the names refer to the sources (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) specified above.

verses.text: In some cases the names of the see are clearly corrupt beyond all recognition.

verses.book: Defence Against the Arians. (Apologia Contra Arianos.)
verses.text: The signatures appended to the canons in the collections of councils, are taken (with certain uncritical adaptations) from the Hilarian list, with the addition, in some copies, of Alexander (3 supra), whose name, therefore, has probably dropped out of the Hilarian text in course of transmission.]
